stratagems.name: Cross the Sea Under Camouflage
stratagems.description: Hide your real intentions behind normal activities.
stratagems.example: Negotiate while pretending it's just a casual chat.

stratagems.name: Besiege Wei to Rescue Zhao
stratagems.description: Attack the enemy's weak point to force them to retreat from their strong position.
stratagems.example: Instead of direct confrontation, strike their supply chain.

stratagems.name: Kill with a Borrowed Knife
stratagems.description: Use someone else's resources or power to achieve your goal.
stratagems.example: Get a third party to pressure your competitor.

stratagems.name: Wait at Leisure While the Enemy Toils
stratagems.description: Conserve your energy while the opponent exhausts themselves.
stratagems.example: Let competitors fight each other while you prepare.

stratagems.name: Loot a Burning House
stratagems.description: Attack when the enemy is in crisis.
stratagems.example: Enter a market when your competitor is having internal problems.

stratagems.name: Make a Sound in the East, Attack in the West
stratagems.description: Create a distraction in one area while striking in another.
stratagems.example: Announce a new feature while launching in a different market.

stratagems.name: Create Something Out of Nothing
stratagems.description: Turn a weak position into strength by creating illusions or opportunities.
stratagems.example: Build hype around a minimum viable product.

stratagems.name: Openly Repair the Gallery, Secretly Cross to Chencang
stratagems.description: Use a visible, harmless action to mask your real plan.
stratagems.example: Publicly focus on one product while secretly developing the next.

stratagems.name: Watch the Fires Burn Across the River
stratagems.description: Stay neutral and watch your enemies destroy each other.
stratagems.example: Let two competitors fight while you strengthen your position.

stratagems.name: Hide a Dagger Behind a Smile
stratagems.description: Appear friendly while preparing a decisive blow.
stratagems.example: Maintain good relations while building a better product.

stratagems.name: Sacrifice the Plum for the Peach
stratagems.description: Give up something small to gain something greater.
stratagems.example: Lose a small client to win a much bigger one.

stratagems.name: Take the Opportunity to Lead the Sheep Away
stratagems.description: Exploit small opportunities as they arise.
stratagems.example: Quickly grab market share when a competitor slips.

stratagems.name: Beat the Grass to Startle the Snake
stratagems.description: Make a small move to test the enemy's reaction.
stratagems.example: Test a small price change to see competitor response.

stratagems.name: Borrow a Corpse to Resurrect the Soul
stratagems.description: Revive an old idea or product in a new form.
stratagems.example: Rebrand an old service for a new market.

stratagems.name: Lure the Tiger Down the Mountain
stratagems.description: Lure the enemy out of their strong position.
stratagems.example: Force a competitor to fight on your terms.

stratagems.name: Let the Enemy Dig Their Own Grave
stratagems.description: Allow the enemy to make mistakes and overextend.
stratagems.example: Wait for competitor to over-invest in a failing strategy.

stratagems.name: Cast a Brick to Attract Jade
stratagems.description: Offer something small to gain something valuable.
stratagems.example: Give free value to attract high-paying clients.

stratagems.name: Catch the Thief by Catching His Leader
stratagems.description: Attack the leader or key person to collapse the group.
stratagems.example: Target the decision-maker in negotiations.

stratagems.name: Remove the Firewood from Under the Pot
stratagems.description: Cut off the enemy's resources or support.
stratagems.example: Disrupt their supply chain or key partnerships.

stratagems.name: Muddy the Waters
stratagems.description: Create confusion to hide your real actions.
stratagems.example: Launch multiple initiatives to confuse competitors.

stratagems.name: Golden Cicada Sheds Its Shell
stratagems.description: Escape danger by leaving a decoy.
stratagems.example: Exit a failing project gracefully while shifting focus.

stratagems.name: Shut the Door to Catch the Thief
stratagems.description: Trap the enemy so they have no escape.
stratagems.example: Create exclusivity in your offer.

stratagems.name: Befriend a Distant Enemy to Attack a Nearby One
stratagems.description: Form alliances with distant parties against a common nearby threat.
stratagems.example: Partner with international players against local competitors.

stratagems.name: Borrow the Road to Attack Guo
stratagems.description: Use a temporary alliance to achieve your goal, then discard it.
stratagems.example: Use a partner channel then build your own.

stratagems.name: Replace the Beams with Rotten Timbers
stratagems.description: Replace key elements subtly to weaken the structure.
stratagems.example: Gradually shift client loyalty from competitor to you.

stratagems.name: Point at the Mulberry and Abuse the Locust
stratagems.description: Criticize one to warn others indirectly.
stratagems.example: Publicly praise a value that indirectly criticizes competitors.

stratagems.name: Feign Madness but Keep Wise
stratagems.description: Pretend weakness or ignorance while staying sharp.
stratagems.example: Act less threatening while preparing a strong move.

stratagems.name: Remove the Ladder After Climbing the Wall
stratagems.description: Cut off your own retreat to force full commitment.
stratagems.example: Burn bridges publicly to commit your team.

stratagems.name: Deck the Tree with Fake Flowers
stratagems.description: Make something weak appear strong.
stratagems.example: Use great marketing for a minimum product.

stratagems.name: Make the Host and the Guest Swap Places
stratagems.description: Turn a defensive position into an offensive one.
stratagems.example: Go from follower to market leader.

stratagems.name: Use the Beauty to Trap the Enemy
stratagems.description: Use allure, incentives, or charm to weaken the enemy.
stratagems.example: Offer irresistible deals to steal clients.

stratagems.name: Open the Gates, Invite the Enemy In
stratagems.description: Appear undefended to lure the enemy into a trap.
stratagems.example: Show weakness publicly while having a strong counter ready.

stratagems.name: Use the Enemy's Agents Against Him
stratagems.description: Turn the enemy's spies or partners against them.
stratagems.example: Hire away their key talent.

stratagems.name: Self-Torture Scheme
stratagems.description: Inflict pain on yourself to gain trust or sympathy.
stratagems.example: Publicly admit a small flaw to build credibility.

stratagems.name: Chain Stratagems
stratagems.description: Link multiple stratagems together for a bigger effect.
stratagems.example: Combine several moves in a long-term campaign.

stratagems.name: Run Away as the Best Strategy
stratagems.description: When all else fails, retreat to fight another day.
stratagems.example: Pivot or exit a failing market gracefully.
